User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the EcoSolar Invest application.

photoURL.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

hasInvested.description: Indicates whether the user has made an investment.

profileCompleted.description: Indicates whether the user has completed their profile.

status.description: The status of the user account (e.g., active, inactive).

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the user was created.

withdrawalAccount.description: The user's primary withdrawal account details.

WithdrawalAccount.title: WithdrawalAccount
WithdrawalAccount.description: Represents a user's bank account for withdrawals.

bankName.description: The name of the user's bank.

accountName.description: The name on the bank account.

country.description: The country of the bank account.

lastUsedAt.description: Timestamp of the last successful withdrawal with this account.

id.description: Unique identifier for the investment plan.

name.description: The name of the investment plan.

investmentAmount.description: The amount required to invest in this plan.

durationDays.description: The duration of the investment plan in days.

dailyProfit.description: The daily profit earned from the investment.

totalReturn.description: The total return from the investment plan.

Investment.title: Investment
Investment.description: Represents a user's investment in a specific plan.

startDate.description: The start date of the investment.

endDate.description: The end date of the investment.

status.description: The status of the investment (e.g., active, completed).

lastProfitCreditedAt.description: The timestamp of the last time profit was credited.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a financial transaction, including withdrawals.

transactionType.description: The type of transaction (e.g., deposit, withdrawal, bonus, profit).

amount.description: The amount of the transaction in the user's currency.

transactionDate.description: The date and time of the transaction.

status.description: The status of the transaction (e.g., pending, completed, failed, rejected).

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification sent to a user.

type.description: The type of notification (e.g., deposit, investment, profit, withdrawal, bonus, error, info).

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the notification was created.

read.description: Indicates whether the notification has been read by the user.

amount.description: Optional monetary amount associated with the notification.

currency.description: Optional currency code for the amount.

WithdrawalRequest.title: WithdrawalRequest
WithdrawalRequest.description: Represents a user's request to withdraw funds, intended for admin review. This entity is now implicitly part of the Transaction entity.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. The userId parameter corresponds to the Firebase Auth UID, ensuring path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

definition.description: Stores user investments. Path-based ownership is enforced via the userId parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

params.name: investmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the investment.

definition.description: Stores user transactions, including withdrawal requests. Path-based ownership is enforced via the userId parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores user notifications. Path-based ownership is enforced via the userId parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique ID of the notification.

definition.description: Stores investment plans, accessible to all authenticated users.

params.name: investmentPlanId
params.description: The unique ID of the investment plan.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed for a single-user security model. All user-specific data (investments, transactions, notifications) is nested within that user's document in a subcollection (e.g., `/users/{userId}/investments/{investmentId}`). This enforces path-based ownership, making security rules simple and effective, as access can be granted by matching the authenticated user's UID (`request.auth.uid`) with the `{userId}` in the document path. Public data like investment plans are stored in a top-level collection. This design is secure, scalable, and prevents data leakage by ensuring users can only ever access their own information.
